body:
Title: CRA 2025 T1 federal non-refundable tax credit aggregation.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package/5000-r.html
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-r/5000-r-25e.txt

CRA Form 5000-R Income Tax and Benefit Return for 2025 Step 5 Part B calculates federal non-refundable tax credits. Lines 77 to 83 include basic personal amount line 30000, age amount line 30100, spouse or common-law partner amount line 30300, amount for an eligible dependant line 30400, Canada caregiver amount for spouse or common-law partner or eligible dependant age 18 or older line 30425, Canada caregiver amount for other infirm dependants age 18 or older line 30450, and Canada caregiver amount for infirm children under 18 line 30500. Line 83 multiplies the number of children on line 30499 by $2,687. Line 84 adds lines 77 to 83.

Lines 86 to 95 include CPP or QPP contributions through employment line 30800, CPP or QPP contributions on self-employment income and other earnings line 31000, employment insurance premiums through employment line 31200, employment insurance premiums on self-employment and other eligible earnings line 31217, volunteer firefighters' amount line 31220, search and rescue volunteers' amount line 31240, Canada employment amount line 31260, home buyers' amount line 31270, home accessibility expenses line 31285, and adoption expenses line 31300. Line 92 calculates the Canada employment amount as the lesser of $1,471 and the listed employment income base. Line 94 caps home accessibility expenses at $20,000. Line 96 adds lines 86 to 95.

Line 97 is pension income amount line 31400, and line 98 adds lines 85, 96, and 97. Line 99 is disability amount for self line 31600, line 100 is disability amount transferred from a dependant line 31800, and line 101 adds lines 98 to 100. Lines 102 to 105 include student loan interest line 31900, federal tuition amount line 32300, tuition amount transferred from a child or grandchild line 32400, and amounts transferred from spouse or common-law partner line 32600. Line 106 adds lines 101 to 105.

Line 107 is medical expenses for self, spouse or common-law partner, and dependent children under 18 line 33099. Line 108 multiplies net income line 23600 by 3%. Line 109 is the lesser of $2,834 and line 108. Line 110 subtracts line 109 from line 107 and floors negative amounts at zero. Line 111 is allowable medical expenses for other dependants line 33199. Line 112 adds line 110 and line 111. Line 113, line 33500, adds line 106 and line 112. Line 114 gives the federal non-refundable tax credit rate as 14.5%. Line 115, line 33800, multiplies line 113 by the rate from line 114. Line 116, line 34900, is donations and gifts. Line 117, line 34990, is the top-up tax credit. Line 118, line 35000, adds lines 115 to 117 as total federal non-refundable tax credits.
